item.description: President Trump was greeted in Beijing with a welcome ceremony ahead of his two-day summit with Chinese President Xi.

media:content.media:title: President Trump arrives in China for high-stakes summit with Xi
media:content.media:description: President Trump was greeted in Beijing with a welcome ceremony ahead of his two-day summit with Chinese President Xi.

item.title: President Trump arrives in China for high-stakes summit with Xi

item.dc:creator: Tom Llamas
Erin Tan
item.description: BEIJING — Old Cheng was waiting patiently at the bottom of a steep mountain ridge as the minivans carrying our news team snaked their way through the Yan mountains to meet him.

media:content.media:text: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.
media:content.media:description: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Top: NBC News' Tom Llamas, center, is guided through the forest beneath the Jiankou section of the Great Wall of China on Tuesday. Bottom: Cheng Yongmao, known as Old Cheng, directs his team of construction workers at the wall.
media:content.media:description: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: The Great Wall in Xiangshuihu, Huairou District on April 22, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall restoration at Xiangshuihu on Tuesday.
media:content.media:description: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Jiankou is one of the most dangerous sections of the Great Wall, known for its steep, often unrestored terrain.
media:content.media:description: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Restoration along the Jiankou section of the Great Wall on Tuesday.
media:content.media:description: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.

item.title: The Great Wall is being restored one brick at a time

item.dc:creator: Tom Llamas
Jennifer Jett
Jay Ganglani
item.description: China loves electric cars: making them, driving them and selling them to the rest of the world.

media:content.media:text: BYC electric vehicles at a showroom in Beijing this week.
media:content.media:description: BYC electric vehicles at a showroom in Beijing this week.

media:content.media:text: China Auto Show
media:content.media:description: A BYD Sealion 05 is displayed during the 2026 Beijing International Automotive Exhibition at the New China International Exhibition Center (NCIEC) in Beijing, China.

media:content.media:text: NBC News' Tom Llamas at a BYD dealership in Beijing this week.
media:content.media:description: NBC News' Tom Llamas at a BYD dealership in Beijing this week.

media:content.media:text: Beijing Auto Show 2026
media:content.media:description: A display of the BYD Denza e3 at the Beijing auto show on April 25. China's premier automotive event showcased the new technologies emerging out of cutthroat competition.

media:content.media:text: BYD's Shenzhen car carrier docked in the port of Suzhou, China on April 17.
media:content.media:description: BYD's Shenzhen car carrier docked in the port of Suzhou, China, on April 17.

item.title: Chinese EVs take the world by storm — except in the U.S.

item.description: How will Trump's China trip differ from his 2017 visit?

media:content.media:title: How will Trump's China trip differ from his 2017 visit?
media:content.media:description: How will Trump's China trip differ from his 2017 visit?

item.title: How will Trump's China trip differ from his 2017 visit?

item.description: Air Force One landed at Beijing Capital International Airport in China. Trump’s state visit includes a bilateral meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. He is the first U.S. president to visit China since his last state visit in 2017 during his first term.

media:content.media:title: Trump arrives in China for Xi summit
media:content.media:description: Air Force One landed at Beijing Capital International Airport in China. Trump’s state visit includes a bilateral meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. He is the first U.S. president to visit China since his last state visit in 2017 during his first term.

item.title: Trump arrives in China for Xi summit

item.description: Here's what's on Trump's to-do list in China

media:content.media:title: Here's what's on Trump's to-do list in China
media:content.media:description: Here's what's on Trump's to-do list in China

item.title: Here's what's on Trump's to-do list in China

item.dc:creator: Babak Dehghanpisheh
item.description: While the Iranian regime has been battling its external enemies, Israel and the United States, it has also been ramping up a deadly crackdown on those seen as the enemy within.

media:content.media:text: Mohammad Amin Biglari
media:content.media:description: The news site of the Iranian judiciary reported on April 5 that Mohammad Amin Biglari, pictured here, was hanged.

media:content.media:text: People gather during protest in Tehran, Iran.
media:content.media:description: The news site of the Iranian judiciary reported on April 5 that Mohammad Amin Biglari, pictured here, was hanged.

media:content.media:text: Image: IRAN-US-ISRAEL-WAR
media:content.media:description: The news site of the Iranian judiciary reported on April 5 that Mohammad Amin Biglari, pictured here, was hanged.

item.title: The war is on hold, but Iran’s internal crackdown is only deepening

item.description: The South Carolina Supreme Court has overturned Alex Murdaugh's double murder conviction and ordered a new trial in the killings of his wife and son. NBC News' Laura Jarrett reports on the court's decision and what comes next for Murdaugh.

media:content.media:title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions and orders new trial
media:content.media:description: The South Carolina Supreme Court has overturned Alex Murdaugh's double murder conviction and ordered a new trial in the killings of his wife and son. NBC News' Laura Jarrett reports on the court's decision and what comes next for Murdaugh.

item.title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions and orders new trial

item.dc:creator: Dennis Romero
Kevin Nious
item.description: The man who pleaded guilty to distributing the ketamine that killed actor Matthew Perry was sentenced to 2 years in federal prison.

media:content.media:text: Matthew Perry's "The End Of Longing" - Photocall
media:content.media:description: Matthew Perry in London, England.

item.title: Drug counselor who distributed ketamine that killed Matthew Perry sentenced to 2 years

item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley
item.description: The Supreme Court has admonished judges not to interfere in election cases when the process is underway, but it is being accused of doing that in redistricting decisions.

media:content.media:text: John Roberts.
media:content.media:description: Chief Justice John Roberts recently complained that Americans view the justices as "political actors."

item.title: Supreme Court faces new criticism for redistricting decision so close to 2026 elections

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Omaha businessman Scott Petersen on Tuesday defeated Nebraska Secretary of State Bob Evnen in a Republican primary election, toppling a two-term incumbent by raising questions on the campaign trail about election integrity and ballot security

media:content.media:text: Bob Evnen.
media:content.media:description: Nebraska Secretary of State Bob Evnen lost in a Republican primary Tuesday night.

item.title: Nebraska secretary of state loses GOP primary to challenger questioning election security

item.dc:creator: Jared Perlo
item.description: OpenEvidence, an AI-powered medical search tool, has become a fast friend to America’s doctors and is now used by nearly two-thirds of physicians.

media:content.media:text: A patient lies in a hospital bed with a pick line attached to their arm, which is connected to a glowing computer chip that is connected to various IV bags and medical screens.
media:content.media:description: Most U.S. doctors are using OpenEvidence to inform medical decision-making.

media:content.media:text: Jeremy Cauwels
media:content.media:description: Most U.S. doctors are using OpenEvidence to inform medical decision-making.

media:content.media:text: OpenEvidence looks similar to many mainstream AI platforms, but tuned for medicine.
media:content.media:description: Most U.S. doctors are using OpenEvidence to inform medical decision-making.

media:content.media:text: Bapu Jena
media:content.media:description: Most U.S. doctors are using OpenEvidence to inform medical decision-making.

media:content.media:text: Dr. Hannah Galvin of Cambridge Health Alliance.
media:content.media:description: Most U.S. doctors are using OpenEvidence to inform medical decision-making.

media:content.media:text: Girish Nadkarni
media:content.media:description: Most U.S. doctors are using OpenEvidence to inform medical decision-making.

item.title: Most U.S. doctors are quietly using this AI tool. Few patients know about it.

item.dc:creator: Bracey Harris
Abigail Brooks
item.description: In a state with a long history of arresting pregnant women, one mom in custody says she was left to fend for herself.

media:content.media:text: Tiffany McElroy.
media:content.media:description: Tiffany McElroy in downtown Dothan, Ala.

media:content.media:text: Tiffany McElroy.
media:content.media:description: Tiffany McElroy in downtown Dothan, Ala.

media:content.media:text: Tiffany McElroy.
media:content.media:description: Tiffany McElroy in downtown Dothan, Ala.

media:content.media:text: Tiffany McElroy.
media:content.media:description: Tiffany McElroy in downtown Dothan, Ala.

item.title: Alabama jail staff didn’t help when she went into labor — other inmates did, lawsuit says

item.description: Sixteen states are reporting potential hantavirus exposures, including Minnesota, Kansas and Washington state. Health officials say most of the cases involve people who left the cruise ship at the center of an outbreak before it was identified or others who traveled with exposed passengers. They say all are asymptomatic and isolating at home. NBC’s Maggie Vespa reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: States Reporting Potential Hantavirus Exposure Grows to 16
media:content.media:description: Sixteen states are reporting potential hantavirus exposures, including Minnesota, Kansas and Washington state. Health officials say most of the cases involve people who left the cruise ship at the center of an outbreak before it was identified or others who traveled with exposed passengers. They say all are asymptomatic and isolating at home. NBC’s Maggie Vespa reports for TODAY.

item.title: States Reporting Potential Hantavirus Exposure Grows to 16

item.description: Rapper and CEO Rick Ross is often inspired by a variety of things: phone calls with his mom, $200 million homes and, of course, 150-foot yachts. He shared some wisdom for NBC News' Business in America.
